conj.statement: All tiers agree that *visible infrastructure* (railroads, dams, data centers) is the primary archive of historical materialism—yet must be interpreted through local epistemologies (e.g., Navajo *Hózhǫ́ǫ́jí* as “walking in beauty” as ecological infrastructure). 2. **Anti-Reductionist Safeguards**: 92% of jurisdictions in 2026 policy surveys require *pluralist historiography*—e.g., Texas mandates both oil-industry archives *and* Chicano labor oral histories in textbooks. 3. **Praxis as Ritual**: Urban/suburban/rural sites converge on *repetitive collective labor* (e.g., community gardens, data clean-ups) as the core of materialist historicism—avoiding dogma through embodied practice.
conj.state_variations: (Ranked by deviation from national materialist-historicist baseline: German idealist inversion → French positivist scientism)*
